question_text: MIS Reports in accounting help in:
explanation: MIS (Management Information System) Reports provide structured, summarised information to management for decision-making. Examples: budget vs actual, cash flow forecast, debtor ageing, stock valuation, expense analysis.
subject: accountancy

options.text: Providing information to management for decision-making

elimination_hints.hint: Journal entries are data input. MIS provides processed output for decisions.
Tax filing is a compliance activity. MIS is for internal management.
Audit is external verification. MIS is for internal information.

question_text: Which of the following is an example of an MIS Report?
explanation: An Ageing Analysis of Debtors shows receivables classified by how long they have been outstanding (e.g., 0-30 days, 31-60 days, 61-90 days, 90+ days). This helps management prioritise collection efforts.
subject: accountancy

elimination_hints.hint: A blank form is for data input, not information output.
Company creation is a setup screen, not a report.
A reference card is user documentation, not a business report.

question_text: Budget vs Actual report in CAS helps management to:
explanation: The Budget vs Actual report compares planned (budgeted) figures with actual results. Variances (differences) are highlighted, enabling management to identify areas of overspending or underperformance and take corrective action.
subject: accountancy

options.text: Identify variances between planned and actual performance

elimination_hints.hint: Transaction recording is data entry, not analysis.
Deleting vouchers is an administrative task, not budgetary analysis.
Creating ledgers is setup. Budget reports are for performance analysis.

question_text: Statement: MIS Reports can be generated for different time periods (daily, weekly, monthly, yearly) in CAS.
explanation: This is TRUE. CAS allows flexible report generation for any time period. Management can view daily cash position, weekly sales, monthly P&L, quarterly comparisons, or annual summaries — all from the same data.
subject: accountancy

elimination_hints.hint: CAS stores date-wise data and can slice it for any period.

question_text: Which of the following best describes the difference between Transaction Processing System (TPS) and MIS?
explanation: TPS handles day-to-day transaction recording (voucher entry, posting). MIS processes the data from TPS and converts it into meaningful reports for management decisions. TPS is operational; MIS is analytical.
subject: accountancy

options.text: TPS and MIS are the same thing
MIS records transactions; TPS generates reports
Both are used only for tax filing
TPS records transactions; MIS converts data into reports for decisions

elimination_hints.hint: They serve different functions — TPS is operational, MIS is analytical.
This is reversed. TPS records; MIS reports.
Tax filing is one small use. TPS and MIS serve broader purposes.

question_text: Stock Summary report in CAS shows:
explanation: Stock Summary shows the current inventory position — items in stock, their quantities, values, and categories. It helps management monitor stock levels, identify slow-moving items, and plan purchases.
subject: accountancy

options.text: Current inventory items, quantities, and values

elimination_hints.hint: Employee salaries are in payroll reports, not stock summary.
Bank reconciliation is a separate report comparing bank and book balances.
Debtor list is in Outstanding report, not Stock Summary.

question_text:
Assertion (A): CAS has largely replaced manual accounting in modern businesses.
Reason (R): CAS offers speed, accuracy, real-time reporting, data security, and easy compliance — benefits that manual systems cannot match.
explanation: Both A and R are true, and R correctly explains A. The overwhelming advantages of CAS (speed, accuracy, automation, compliance features) have made it the standard for modern businesses. Manual accounting is now limited to very small enterprises.
subject: accountancy

options.text: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

elimination_hints.hint: R directly lists the reasons WHY CAS replaced manual systems.
R lists real, verified advantages of CAS — it is factually true.
A is correct — CAS is indeed the dominant system in modern businesses.
elimination_hints.misconception: Thinking manual accounting is still dominant.

question_text: GST (Goods and Services Tax) returns can be directly generated from accounting data in ______ software.
explanation: Tally (Tally Prime) has built-in GST compliance features. It can generate GSTR-1 (sales), GSTR-2 (purchases), GSTR-3B (summary), and other GST returns directly from the accounting data entered through vouchers.
subject: accountancy

elimination_hints.hint: Photoshop is for image editing, not accounting or GST.
PowerPoint is for presentations, not accounting.
Notepad is a text editor with no accounting features.

question_text: Data analysis in CAS can include:
explanation: CAS enables various data analyses: trend analysis (comparing periods), ratio analysis (financial health), ageing analysis (receivables/payables), budget variance analysis, and cost analysis. All are generated from the same underlying data.
subject: accountancy

options.text: Trend analysis, ratio analysis, ageing analysis, budget variance, and more

elimination_hints.hint: CAS supports many types of analysis, not just ratios.
Budget analysis is one type. CAS offers trend, ratio, ageing, and more.
Cash flow is one type. CAS offers comprehensive analysis capabilities.

question_text: The main difference between data and information in the context of CAS is:
explanation: Data consists of raw, unprocessed facts (e.g., individual transaction amounts). Information is processed, organised data that is meaningful and useful for decision-making (e.g., monthly profit trend, debtor ageing report). CAS converts data into information.
subject: accountancy

options.text: Data is raw/unprocessed; Information is processed data useful for decisions
Data and information are the same thing
Information is entered first; data is generated later
Data is always numeric; information is always text

elimination_hints.hint: They are different — data is raw input; information is meaningful output.
It's the reverse — data is entered first, then processed into information.
Both data and information can be numeric or textual.

question_text: Which of the following is a key feature of modern accounting software like Tally Prime?
explanation: Multi-user access allows multiple users to work on the same company data simultaneously — e.g., one person enters sales while another records purchases. This improves efficiency in larger organisations.
subject: accountancy

options.text: Can only be used by one person at a time
Multi-user access for simultaneous data entry
Requires manual calculation of all totals

elimination_hints.hint: Modern CAS supports multiple users — that's a key feature.
Tally Prime has extensive GST compliance features.
Automatic calculation is a core CAS feature — no manual totalling needed.

question_text: Exception Reports in MIS are reports that:
explanation: Exception reports highlight items that deviate from normal patterns or exceed predefined thresholds — e.g., expenses exceeding budget by more than 10%, overdue receivables beyond 90 days, or unusually large transactions. They help management focus on problem areas.
subject: accountancy

options.text: Show all transactions without any filter
Highlight deviations from normal patterns or thresholds

elimination_hints.hint: Showing everything is a detailed report, not an exception report.
Exception reports can be generated at any frequency — daily, weekly, etc.
Exception reports focus on UNUSUAL items, not routine successful ones.
elimination_hints.misconception: Focusing on normal instead of abnormal.

question_text: Statement: In CAS, the same transaction data entered once can be used to generate multiple reports (TB, P&L, B/S, Cash Flow, Ratios).
explanation: This is TRUE. One of the biggest advantages of CAS is that data is entered ONCE (through vouchers) and the system generates ALL reports from that single source — Trial Balance, P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Ratios, MIS reports, and GST returns.
subject: accountancy

elimination_hints.hint: CAS eliminates re-entry — one voucher updates all reports automatically.
elimination_hints.misconception: Thinking separate data entry is needed for each report.

question_text: Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) in CAS helps in:
explanation: BRS in CAS automatically compares the bank ledger in the books with the bank statement. It identifies differences (cheques issued but not cleared, deposits not yet credited) and helps reconcile the two balances.
subject: accountancy

options.text: Matching book balance with bank statement balance

elimination_hints.hint: Account creation is setup, not reconciliation.
Interest calculation is a separate function, not BRS.
Recording withdrawals is data entry. BRS is a verification tool.

question_text: Which of the following statements about CAS is INCORRECT?
explanation: CAS does NOT eliminate the need for human judgement. While it automates calculations and report generation, accounting estimates (bad debts provision, useful life of assets, inventory valuation method) still require professional judgement.
subject: accountancy

options.text: CAS reduces manual errors in calculations
CAS can generate multiple reports from the same data
CAS completely eliminates the need for human judgement in accounting
CAS requires trained personnel to operate

elimination_hints.hint: This is correct — CAS does reduce manual errors. Not the incorrect statement.
This is correct — single entry, multiple reports. Not the incorrect statement.
This is correct — trained staff are needed. Not the incorrect statement.

question_text: In CAS, 'Data Export' feature allows:
explanation: Data Export allows accounting data to be exported in various formats (Excel, PDF, XML, CSV) for sharing with auditors, tax consultants, or for further analysis in other software. This interoperability is a key CAS feature.
subject: accountancy

options.text: Saving accounting data in formats like Excel, PDF for sharing
Printing only in black and white

elimination_hints.hint: Export is about copying data out, not deleting it.
Image conversion is not an accounting function.
Printing settings are separate from data export.

question_text: Which of the following is NOT a function of accounting software?
explanation: Video editing is not a function of accounting software. Accounting software handles recording transactions, generating reports, managing inventory, tax compliance (GST), and payroll — all financial/business functions.
subject: accountancy

elimination_hints.hint: Recording transactions IS a core function of accounting software.
Balance Sheet generation IS a key output of accounting software.
GST compliance IS a built-in feature of modern accounting software.

question_text: Cloud-based accounting software offers the advantage of:
explanation: Cloud-based accounting (like QuickBooks Online, Zoho Books) allows access from anywhere via internet — from office, home, or while travelling. Data is stored on remote servers, enabling real-time collaboration and automatic backups.
subject: accountancy

options.text: Access from anywhere via internet, real-time collaboration
Data can only be accessed from one computer
No internet connection is ever needed
It is always free of cost

elimination_hints.hint: Anywhere access is the whole point of cloud computing.
Cloud-based means internet is required for access.
Cloud software typically requires subscription fees.
elimination_hints.misconception: Assuming all cloud services are free.

question_text: Statement: Accounting software can help in statutory compliance such as GST filing, TDS returns, and e-filing of income tax.
explanation: This is TRUE. Modern accounting software like Tally Prime includes modules for GST compliance (GSTR-1, GSTR-3B), TDS computation and returns, and can generate data for income tax e-filing. This reduces compliance burden significantly.
subject: accountancy

elimination_hints.hint: Modern CAS absolutely supports GST, TDS, and tax compliance features.

question_text:
Match the MIS report types with their descriptions:

Column I:
(i) Ageing Analysis
(ii) Budget vs Actual
(iii) Exception Report
(iv) Stock Summary

Column II:
(P) Shows current inventory quantities and values
(Q) Highlights items deviating from normal thresholds
(R) Compares planned vs actual financial performance
(S) Classifies receivables/payables by overdue period
explanation: Ageing Analysis → Classifies by overdue period (S). Budget vs Actual → Planned vs actual (R). Exception Report → Deviations from thresholds (Q). Stock Summary → Inventory quantities and values (P).
subject: accountancy

elimination_hints.hint: Ageing Analysis classifies by overdue period (S), not planned vs actual (R).
Budget vs Actual compares planned/actual (R), not highlights deviations (Q).
Ageing Analysis is about overdue periods (S), not deviations (Q).
